Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for BrightLoop May expansion using search_emails (queries: "BrightLoop", "Tessa Moreno", "six relocations", "pricing"). Discover Tessa's expansion email (email_email_4ea76cf653a9) with 6 employees, Mina's own phased intake response (email_email_b10087d5aaf0), Marcus Thorne's pricing guardrails email (email_email_23e2c0b9f535), and Alejandro's pricing gap analysis (email_email_c7daa25c832d).Read or lookup. search_emails is default then skip TS, skip QC.
OE 2: Search emails for Vectral renewal using search_emails (queries: "Vectral", "Rachel Whitfield", "renewal", "board"). Discover the counter-proposal history (more than 10% sweeteners delivered April 19), Rachel's 12% counter-demand, and the board meeting ~April 23 with no response since.Read or lookup. search_emails is default then skip TS, skip QC.
OE 3: Search emails for Canopy Health housing using search_emails (queries: "Canopy", "Dr. Mehta", "Anand Mehta", "housing", "UrbanNest"). Discover Fatimah's 3 housing options email to Dr. Mehta (email_email_34ce8e435545) with the Streeterville recommendation, Carmen Reyes' UrbanNest options (email_email_2a9a1d2f91b6) with 48-hour holds, and Fatimah's April 25 deadline.Read or lookup. search_emails is default then skip TS, skip QC.
OE 4: Search Slack for internal discussions using conversations_search_messages (queries: "BrightLoop pricing", "Vectral renewal", "Mehta housing", "international executive"). Discover Marcus Thorne's pricing guardrails (no blended quotes), Alejandro's tier analysis, Chloe's capacity concerns, and any internal context on Rachel's silence. Read or lookup. conversations_search_messages is default then skip TS, skip QC.
OE 5: Search CRM for all 3 client accounts using crm_search_companies (names: "BrightLoop", "Vectral", "Canopy"). Discover company records, deal stages, and account context. Read or lookup. crm_search_companies is non-default and query obvious then TS yes, QC no.
OE 6: Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals (by company_id for each client). Discover Vectral renewal deal stage (at_risk), Canopy Dr. Mehta deal ($12,170), and BrightLoop deal status. Read or lookup. crm_search_deals is non-default and requires company_id then TS yes, QC yes.
OE 7: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements (company_ids for each client). Discover Vectral renewal engagement notes (engagement_vectral_renewal_negotiation), Canopy executive relocation engagement (crm_engagement_ac101931ed37), and BrightLoop engagement history. Read or lookup. crm_list_engagements is non-default and requires company_id then TS yes, QC yes.
OE 8: Search Airtable for relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table: "Relocations", field: "Company", values per client). Check if BrightLoop May relocations are in the system, verify Canopy Dr. Mehta relocation status, and check BrightLoop April batch status (Simone Richter, Marcus Webb). Read or lookup. airtable_search_records is non-default then skip TS, skip QC.
OE 9: Search QuickBooks for billing using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria for Vectral, Canopy, BrightLoop). Discover Vectral renewal estimate EST-2026-0419 ($58,400), Canopy Dr. Mehta estimate EST-2026-0422 ($12,170), and BrightLoop invoices. Read or lookup. quickbooks_search_invoices is non-default and non-trivial then TS yes, QC yes.
OE 10: Look up contacts using contacts_search_contacts (queries: "Tessa Moreno", "Rachel Whitfield", "Dr. Mehta"). Find email addresses for sending client emails. Read or lookup. contacts_search_contacts is default then skip TS, skip QC.
OE 11: Send email to Rachel Whitfield at Vectral using send_email (recipient: rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com). Warm check-in on the board outcome after the renewal counter-offer. Write action then Outcome rubric.
Outcome 1.1: "send_email was called with recipient rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com."
OE 12: Send email to Dr. Anand Mehta at Canopy using send_email (recipient: anand.mehta@canopyhealth.io). Urgent follow-up on housing selection with holds expiring. Write action then Outcome rubric.
Outcome 1.1: "send_email was called with recipient anand.mehta@canopyhealth.io."
OE 13: Send email to Tessa Moreno at BrightLoop using send_email (recipient: tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com). Update on pricing status standard domestic quotable, international/executive pending finance. Write action then Outcome rubric.
Outcome 1.1: "send_email was called with recipient tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com."
OE 14: Log CRM engagements using crm_create_engagement. Create engagement notes for each account documenting current status so David has visibility. Write action then Outcome rubric.
Outcome 1.1: "crm_create_engagement was called at least once in the trajectory."
OE 15: Agent discovers and reports that Marcus Thorne's pricing guardrails are blocking the BrightLoop quote no international or executive pricing without finance sign-off (or similar).
Outcome 2.1: "The final response mentions Marcus Thorne's pricing guardrails or finance sign-off blocking BrightLoop pricing (or similar)."
OE 16: Agent discovers and reports the Vectral renewal counter-proposal details more than 10% discount and sweeteners, with Rachel's board having met ~April 23 and no response since (or similar).
Outcome 2.1: "The final response mentions the Vectral 10% counter-offer and board silence (or similar)."
OE 17: Agent discovers and reports that the Canopy housing holds have expired or are about to expire (or similar).
Outcome 2.1: "The final response mentions Canopy housing holds expired or expiring (or similar)."
OE 18: Agent discovers and reports the Streeterville/Pinnacle option as the recommended housing for Dr. Mehta (or similar).
Outcome 2.1: "The final response mentions Option B Streeterville or The Pinnacle as recommended housing (or similar)."
OE 19: Agent discovers and reports that BrightLoop has 3 standard domestic relocations quotable now but 2 (Oliver Bancroft international, Jonathan Marsh executive) are blocked on pricing (or similar).
Outcome 2.1: "The final response distinguishes standard domestic as quotable vs international/executive as blocked (or similar)."
OE 20: Agent discovers and reports the Vectral renewal gap is approximately $720/year or 2% (or similar detail showing the gap is small).
Outcome 2.1: "The final response mentions the Vectral renewal gap is approximately $720 or 2% (or similar)."
OE 21: Agent discovers and reports that David contacted Meridian Global Mobility for UK partner costs but hasn't received a response (or similar).
Outcome 2.1: "The final response mentions Meridian Global Mobility or the UK partner costs inquiry with no response (or similar)."

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

5 The final response mentions Marcus Thorne's pricing guardrails blocking the BrightLoop quote (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response explicitly states: 'No evidence anywhere in email, Slack, or CRM that Marcus Thorne or Alejandro Fuentes has been asked for or provided pricing on these two cases. The blocker is purely internal.' and that an urgent email was sent to Marcus Thorne requesting pricing sign-off.
✓Run #2The final response explicitly states 'Marcus Thorne issued explicit guardrails on April 22' blocking international and executive pricing for BrightLoop.
✓Run #3The final response explicitly states 'Marcus Thorne explicitly set guardrails that no international or executive-tier pricing can go to Tessa without finance approval.' This clearly identifies the pricing guardrails blocking the BrightLoop quote.
✓Run #4The final response explicitly states 'Marcus Thorne explicitly blocked any international (Oliver Bancroft) or executive white-glove (Jonathan Marsh) quotes from going to Tessa until...' and mentions finance sign-off requirement.
✓Run #5The final response explicitly mentions 'Marcus Thorne and Alejandro Fuentes confirmed' the pricing limitations and quotes Marcus's direct instruction: 'do not promise all-in package pricing.' Finance sign-off as the blocker is clearly described.
✓Run #6The final response states: 'Marcus explicitly blocked any quote until we define the service scope line-by-line and get finance approval.' This clearly describes Marcus Thorne's pricing guardrails blocking the BrightLoop quote.
6 The final response distinguishes standard domestic relocations as quotable now versus international and executive as blocked (or similar)
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response clearly distinguishes the four standard domestic cases (Priya Naidu, Leah Kwon, Daniel Okafor, Sofia Reyes) that 'can be priced at standard domestic rates' from Oliver Bancroft (international) and Jonathan Marsh (executive-tier) which are blocked.
✓Run #2The final response explicitly distinguishes the three standard domestic moves (Leah Kwon, Daniel Okafor, Sofia Reyes) as quotable now, while Oliver Bancroft (international) and Jonathan Marsh (executive) are hard blocked.
✓Run #3The final response clearly distinguishes: '3 standard domestic moves (Leah, Daniel, Sofia) — can be priced from the existing tier model' vs Oliver Bancroft (international) and Jonathan Marsh (executive CTO) both flagged as BLOCKED.
✓Run #4The final response clearly distinguishes: 'The 3 standard domestic moves (Leah, Daniel, Sofia) CAN be quoted using existing Terraform-model tier logic' while international (Oliver Bancroft) and executive (Jonathan Marsh) are blocked.
✓Run #5The final response clearly distinguishes the 3 standard domestic moves (Leah Kwon, Daniel Okafor, Sofia Reyes) as 'ready to quote' while Oliver Bancroft (international) and Jonathan Marsh (executive) are 'explicitly blocked.'
✓Run #6The final response explicitly distinguishes: 3 standard domestic moves (Leah Kwon, Daniel Okafor, Sofia Reyes) are 'conceptually ready' but need sign-off, while international (Oliver Bancroft) and executive white-glove (Jonathan Marsh) are fully blocked.
